label: Compliance Ontology
notes: Volume XI Sprint-1 pack (ADR-0014/0015). Operational ontology for the financial-services compliance program — customer due diligence (KYC/KYB/CDD/EDD), BSA/AML sanctions and reporting (OFAC/SAR/CTR), consumer-lending disclosure and fair-lending regimes (TILA/RESPA/TRID/HMDA/CRA/UDAAP), privacy/consent (GLBA/privacy/consent), and the audit/review controls that evidence them. Enriches Object Registry classes by schema_ref with relationships, lifecycle, KPIs, required documents, and connectors. bsa is defined in the credit_union anchor pack and is not redefined here; it is referenced as a closed target. Relationship targets are catalog schema_refs (closed graph). Never place a compliance conclusion in weights alone: each finding persists evidence, source, confidence, and lineage (DOCTRINE truth hierarchy; ADR-0005). Connector keys are forward references into the Connector Registry (Volume IX).

ontologies.label: KYC (Know Your Customer / CIP)
ontologies.notes: Customer Identification Program under the BSA (31 CFR 1020.220): collect, verify, and retain identifying information for natural-person members/customers, and screen against sanctions/PEP lists at onboarding.

relationships.description: Run at application before an account is opened.
CIP is the identification leg of Customer Due Diligence.

required_documents.label: CIP Identity Record (name, DOB, address, TIN)

ontologies.label: KYB (Know Your Business)
ontologies.notes: Business/entity due diligence and beneficial-ownership identification under the FinCEN CDD Rule (31 CFR 1010.230): identify and verify each beneficial owner (>=25%) and one control person.

relationships.description: Legal-entity customers (business members, CUSOs, fintech partners).

ontologies.label: AML (Anti-Money-Laundering Program)
ontologies.notes: The four-pillar-plus AML program required under the BSA: internal controls, designated BSA officer, training, independent testing, and risk-based CDD. Detects and reports suspicious activity via transaction monitoring.

relationships.description: Ongoing transaction monitoring of member activity.
Alerts become investigation cases; evidence + lineage persist per assertion, not weights.

ontologies.label: OFAC (Sanctions Screening)
ontologies.notes: Office of Foreign Assets Control sanctions compliance: screen parties/transactions against SDN and consolidated sanctions lists; block or reject and report to OFAC. Strict-liability regime.

relationships.description: Potential matches become investigation cases before any block/reject decision.

ontologies.label: CDD (Customer Due Diligence)
ontologies.notes: Risk-based customer due diligence under the FinCEN CDD Rule: understand the nature/purpose of the relationship, develop a risk profile, and conduct ongoing monitoring. EDD applies to higher-risk relationships.

ontologies.label: EDD (Enhanced Due Diligence)
ontologies.notes: Enhanced due diligence for higher-risk relationships (PEPs, high-risk geographies, MSBs, correspondent relationships, cash-intensive businesses): additional information, senior approval, and heightened ongoing monitoring.

relationships.description: Senior management sign-off for high-risk relationships.

ontologies.label: SAR (Suspicious Activity Report)
ontologies.notes: FinCEN Suspicious Activity Report: file within 30 days of initial detection (60 with no identified suspect). Confidential — unlawful to disclose. Persist detection evidence and lineage; the filing decision is human-approved, not weights-only.

relationships.description: Filed to FinCEN via BSA E-Filing; examined by the prudential regulator (NCUA/FDIC/OCC).

ontologies.label: CTR (Currency Transaction Report)
ontologies.notes: FinCEN Currency Transaction Report: file for cash transactions aggregating more than $10,000 by or on behalf of one person in a business day. File within 15 days. Non-discretionary once the threshold is met.

relationships.description: Filed to FinCEN via BSA E-Filing.

ontologies.label: TRID (TILA-RESPA Integrated Disclosure)
ontologies.notes: TILA-RESPA Integrated Disclosure rule (Reg Z / Reg X): the Loan Estimate within 3 business days of application and the Closing Disclosure at least 3 business days before consummation, with tolerance rules on fee changes.

ontologies.label: RESPA (Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act)
ontologies.notes: Reg X: settlement-cost disclosure, prohibition on kickbacks/referral fees (Section 8), escrow-account limits, and mortgage-servicing/loss-mitigation requirements.

ontologies.label: TILA (Truth in Lending Act)
ontologies.notes: Reg Z: uniform disclosure of the cost of consumer credit (APR, finance charge, amount financed), ability-to-repay/QM for mortgages, right of rescission, and advertising rules.

ontologies.label: HMDA (Home Mortgage Disclosure Act)
ontologies.notes: Reg C: collect and report loan-level mortgage application data (the LAR) to the CFPB/FFIEC to identify fair-lending and community-credit patterns. Annual submission by March 1.

relationships.description: HMDA data supports fair-lending and CRA analysis.

ontologies.label: CRA (Community Reinvestment Act)
ontologies.notes: For banks/thrifts (FDIC/OCC/FRB): assess how the institution meets the credit needs of its entire community, including LMI areas. Credit unions are not subject to federal CRA but many states impose analogous obligations.

kpis.label: CRA Rating
kpis.threshold: Outstanding / Satisfactory / Needs to Improve / Substantial Noncompliance

ontologies.label: GLBA (Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act)
ontologies.notes: Consumer financial privacy: the Privacy Rule (initial/annual notices, opt-out of sharing with nonaffiliated third parties) and the Safeguards Rule (information security program to protect nonpublic personal information).

ontologies.label: UDAAP (Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices)
ontologies.notes: Dodd-Frank Section 1031/1036 (and FTC Act UDAP): prohibits unfair, deceptive, or abusive acts across marketing, servicing, fees, and complaint handling. Enforced principally by the CFPB.

relationships.description: Complaint patterns become review cases; findings persist with evidence + lineage.

ontologies.label: Privacy (Data Privacy Program)
ontologies.notes: The institution's consumer-data privacy program spanning GLBA, state privacy laws (CCPA/CPRA and successors), data-subject requests, data mapping, and retention. Distinct from the GLBA statutory object: this is the operating program.

ontologies.label: Consent (Consent & Authorization Management)
ontologies.notes: Capture, evidence, and honor consumer consents and authorizations: E-SIGN electronic-records consent, Reg E EFT authorizations, TCPA marketing consent, GLBA opt-outs, and Reg DD/Z e-disclosure consent. Every consent persists with source, timestamp, and lineage.

relationships.description: E-SIGN consent gates electronic delivery of disclosures.

ontologies.label: Audit (Independent / Internal Audit)
ontologies.notes: The independent audit function providing assurance over controls: internal audit plan, external financial-statement audit (NCUA supervisory-committee audit for CUs), and independent BSA/AML testing. Findings drive remediation.

relationships.description: Findings become tracked remediation items with owners.

ontologies.label: Compliance Review (Compliance Management System)
ontologies.notes: The compliance management system's monitoring/review cycle: risk assessment, control monitoring, transaction/file testing, issue management, and board/committee reporting across the consumer-compliance and BSA regimes.
